questions.question: Which of the following color spaces is suitable for printing images?
questions.explanation: CMYK is a color model used for printing, as it takes into account the colorant properties and the way they interact with each other.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the YCbCr color space in image processing?
options: A) To convert RGB images to CMYK
B) To separate the luminance and chrominance information
C) To create a new color model
D) To reduce the color gamut
questions.explanation: YCbCr separates the luminance (Y) and chrominance (Cb and Cr) information, which is useful for image compression and processing.

questions.question: Which color image sharpening technique involves calculating the gradient of the image and adding it back to the original image?
questions.explanation: Unsharp Masking involves calculating the gradient of the image and adding it back to the original image to enhance details and textures.

questions.question: What is the purpose of color segmentation in image processing?
options: A) To reduce noise in images
C) To separate objects of similar colors
D) To create a new color model
questions.explanation: Color segmentation is used to separate objects of similar colors, which is useful for applications such as object recognition and image classification.

questions.question: What type of noise is most commonly found in color images?
questions.explanation: Salt and Pepper Noise is a type of noise that consists of random white or black pixels scattered throughout the image, which can be found in color images as well as black and white images.
